figures.caption: Fig. 5. Main convergence comparison between TiLP and representative FSL baselines, including ASFL [9], FedSL [38], and HSFL [10].
TABLE III MAIN RESULTS OF TILP AGAINST EIGHT FSL BASELINES. BEST IN RED BOLD, RUNNER-UP IN BLUE; “–” DENOTES AN UNREACHED THRESHOLD OR AN UNDEFINED METRIC.

TABLE IV ABLATION STUDIES OF TILP. “W/O NETWORK”, “W/O TRAINING”, AND “W/O TASK” FREEZE CALIBRATION LOOP 1, LOOP 2, AND LOOP 3 RESPECTIVELY WHILE KEEPING ALL SUB-TWINS ACTIVE; “W/O CALIBRATION” FREEZES ALL THREE LOOPS. “W/O CEM” BYPASSES CEM AND OUTPUTS THE ACTOR’S MEAN DIRECTLY. “W/O TASK-DRIVEN” REPLACES THE TASK-SUCCESS REWARD WITH THE NEGATIVE TRAINING-LOSS DECREMENT, LEAVING TWIN AND PLANNER INTACT. “–” DENOTES AN UNREACHED THRESHOLD.

Fig. 4. Hardware testbed: HPC (Two H100 GPUs, training sub-twin), AGX2 (network sub-twin, USRP-2900/2955 and Sionna RT), and AGX1 (task subtwin, HighTorque arms, RealSense D435 / D405, MuJoCo), bridged by gigabit Ethernet.
